en: If your company performs any of the following services and is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) for those services, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.
Italy Self-Certification of Technical Professional Requirements Applicability Assessment

The Italy Self-Certification of Technical Professional Requirements is a formal written declaration by a business, contractor, or self-employed professional confirming possession of the technical competence and professional qualifications required to perform regulated activities under Italian law- most commonly in the construction, facility management, and occupational safety sectors. It serves as evidence of compliance with statutory obligations related to workplace safety (Legislative Decree 81) and technical system requirements (Ministerial Decree 37), allowing affirmation that all applicable standards and qualifications have been met when carrying out works, services, or supplies subject to health, safety, and technical compliance oversight.\n\nPresidential Decree 445 Article 47

Is your company required to hold an Italian Self-Certification of Technical-Professional Requirements in accordance with Article 19 of Law No. 241 and Articles 3 and 4 of Ministerial Decree No. 37, as administered by the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy (MIMIT)?
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing an Italy Self-Certification of Technical Professional Requirements.

The company does not carry out activities listed as regulated or requiring technical qualifications under Italian legislation such as Ministerial Decree 37 or Legislative Decree 81- e.g. construction, facility management, and plant installation.
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing an Italy Self-Certification of Technical Professional Requirements.
By providing your electronic signature, you confirm that the information submitted in this questionnaire is true, complete, and accurate in accordance with Italian legislation.
